title: Rivian Q2 2023 Earnings Report
text: Rivian reported Q2 2023 earnings with a net loss of $1.2 billion, improved from $1.71 billion in Q2 2022. Revenue reached $1.12 billion, exceeded the $1 billion estimate and up from $364 million year-over-year. Adjusted loss per share was $1.08, better than $1.41 expected. Production and deliveries each grew approximately 50-60% quarter-over-quarter. Gross profit per vehicle improved by roughly $35,000. The R1S model represented 70% of R1 production for the first time. The company successfully introduced in-house Enduro motor to R1 platform. Updated 2023 guidance included production target increased to 52,000 units from previous 50,000, adjusted EBITDA guidance improved to $(4.2) billion, and capital expenditures reduced to $1.7 billion. RIVN closed up $0.52 per share on the day, though declined in after-hours trading.
